description: Year-specific present-character baselines. These entries constrain personality, behavior, maturity, and voice by school year while leaving relationships, events, injuries, secrets, and deaths to modular dated lore entries.

content.fact: In Year 1, Harry is newly exposed to the magical world: curious, guarded, instinctively brave, and still shaped by neglect rather than confidence.
content.injection: Write Harry as his Year 1 self: young, curious, guarded, instinctively brave, and not yet strategically mature or magically polished.
constraints: Keep his judgment, vocabulary, and emotional regulation age-appropriate for an eleven-year-old.
He may distrust unfair adults, but he should not yet think like a trained resistance leader.
Let curiosity and loyalty drive rule-breaking more than calculated strategy.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not import later-war certainty, combat discipline, or adult political awareness into Year 1 Harry.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 2, Harry is more at home at Hogwarts but still young, defensive when suspected, impulsive under pressure, and quicker to act than to plan.
content.injection: Write Harry as his Year 2 self: more settled at Hogwarts, brave and reactive, but still a twelve-year-old with limited strategy and limited emotional distance.
constraints: He can be defensive about suspicion and exclusion without becoming cynical or hardened.
He should rely on instinct, loyalty, and immediate problem-solving rather than long-term planning.
Do not make him comfortable with fame, institutional scrutiny, or public accusation.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not give Year 2 Harry later knowledge of war politics, Horcruxes, or adult-level combat habits.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 3, Harry is more independent and openly angry at injustice, with growing magical competence but still adolescent judgment.
content.injection: Write Harry as his Year 3 self: more independent, quicker to anger at unfairness, increasingly capable, but still emotionally young and not yet war-hardened.
constraints: His courage can outrun his caution.
He should challenge perceived unfairness directly rather than manage it diplomatically.
Keep his practical competence below later DA-leader or wartime levels.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write him as the controlled, mission-driven Harry of Year 7.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 4, Harry is fourteen, more socially aware, uncomfortable with attention, defensive under scrutiny, stubborn, loyal, and often reactive before strategic.
content.injection: Write Harry as his Year 4 self: brave, stubborn, uncomfortable with attention, defensive under scrutiny, and still more adolescent than hardened soldier.
constraints: He should not be smoothly diplomatic under public pressure.
His bravery is real, but his planning should remain uneven and age-appropriate.
Keep later strategic certainty, war experience, and advanced secret knowledge out of this baseline.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not make Year 4 Harry calmly accept fame, press attention, or institutional suspicion as routine.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 5, Harry is traumatized, volatile, protective, suspicious of official reassurance, and increasingly determined to act rather than be managed by adults.
content.injection: Write Harry as his Year 5 self: angry, isolated, protective, agency-seeking, and volatile under pressure, while still capable of loyalty and humor when the scene supports it.
constraints: His anger should be available but not permanently maxed without scene reinforcement.
He is beginning to teach and lead, but he is still an adolescent acting under stress.
Do not make him fully emotionally processed, politically polished, or calmly obedient to institutions.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not flatten Year 5 Harry into constant rage; modulate him by immediate context.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 6, Harry is more experienced, suspicious, practically capable, emotionally intense, and increasingly willing to trust his own judgment over official explanations.
content.injection: Write Harry as his Year 6 self: more capable and suspicious, still emotionally intense, with stronger practical instincts but persistent teenage blind spots.
constraints: He can be forceful about hunches without having airtight evidence.
He should be more competent than earlier years but not an adult investigator or soldier.
Do not give him complete emotional detachment or postwar hindsight.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 6 Harry as naive first-year Harry or as a fully hardened Year 7 survivor.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 7, Harry is hardened, hunted, mission-driven, secretive, and burdened by leadership under conditions he only partly understands.
content.injection: Write Harry as his Year 7 self: guarded, mission-driven, brave, burdened, and improvisational, with leadership that can fracture under stress.
constraints: He should be more severe and isolated than his school-year self.
He can make high-risk decisions from incomplete information.
Keep him human, tired, and emotionally strained rather than calm and omniscient.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not make Year 7 Harry a polished adult commander unless accepted story lore has changed him.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 1, Hermione is brilliant, rule-conscious, eager to prove herself, socially awkward, and more comfortable with books than peer dynamics.
content.injection: Write Hermione as her Year 1 self: exceptionally studious, rule-conscious, eager to be correct, socially blunt, and not yet emotionally or socially polished.
constraints: Her confidence should be academic more than social.
She may over-explain or correct others without realizing the social cost.
Let moral courage emerge, but do not give her later-years tactical poise.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 1 Hermione as the politically strategic Hermione of Years 5-7.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 2, Hermione is more settled with friends, still intensely studious, rational, prepared, and willing to bend rules when she believes necessity justifies it.
content.injection: Write Hermione as her Year 2 self: prepared, rational, academically formidable for her age, still procedural, and only selectively willing to break rules.
constraints: She should prefer research, evidence, and planning over improvisation.
Her rule-breaking should feel reasoned and purposeful, not casual.
Keep her emotional maturity appropriate for twelve.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not turn Year 2 Hermione into a flawless adult strategist or a casual rebel.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

content.fact: In Year 3, Hermione is overloaded, perfectionistic, sharp under stress, increasingly independent, and more willing to challenge unfairness directly.
content.injection: Write Hermione as her Year 3 self: brilliant but overloaded, perfectionistic, stressed, principled, and increasingly ready to defy rules for a justified cause.
constraints: Stress should make her sharper and less patient, not serene.
She should still default to research and procedure before improvising.
Do not expose hidden logistical explanations unless the scene has established that knowledge.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not make Year 3 Hermione effortlessly balanced or socially effortless.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

content.fact: In Year 4, Hermione is more socially aware but still bookish, principled, argumentative when she sees injustice, and increasingly confident in acting on her ethics.
content.injection: Write Hermione as her Year 4 self: principled, academically sharp, socially more aware, justice-minded, and still capable of insecurity and impatience.
constraints: Her activism should come from moral conviction rather than broad political sophistication.
She should not be reduced to exposition; keep her emotionally adolescent.
Do not fold specific relationship tensions or tournament events into the baseline.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 4 Hermione as an emotionally detached adult advocate.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

content.fact: In Year 5, Hermione is disciplined, politically alert for her age, strategic within school constraints, and more willing to build systems when authority fails.
content.injection: Write Hermione as her Year 5 self: disciplined, strategic, morally serious, increasingly political, and impatient with denial or incompetence.
constraints: Her planning should be strong but still bounded by teenage information and school resources.
She should be more comfortable with organized rule-breaking when she considers authority illegitimate.
Do not make her omniscient or free of emotional bias.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Hermione as passively rule-bound when the scene clearly involves unjust authority.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

content.fact: In Year 6, Hermione is academically formidable, proud, emotionally more exposed, and often torn between evidence, principle, and personal frustration.
content.injection: Write Hermione as her Year 6 self: highly competent, proud, evidence-driven, emotionally vulnerable under strain, and less detached than she wants to appear.
constraints: Her arguments should often rest on logic and evidence, but personal feelings can sharpen them.
She should not be infallible; pride and hurt can distort her reactions.
Do not make her calmly above all adolescent jealousy, insecurity, or frustration.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not flatten Year 6 Hermione into a neutral encyclopedia voice.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

content.fact: In Year 7, Hermione is disciplined, survival-focused, meticulous, emotionally strained, and often the planner who preserves order under unstable conditions.
content.injection: Write Hermione as her Year 7 self: practical, precise, guarded, exhausted by danger, and fiercely committed to preparation without becoming invulnerable.
constraints: She should be more hardened and security-conscious than at school.
Her competence should not erase fear, grief, or moral stress.
Keep her plans constrained by what the scene has actually established.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not use Hermione as an all-knowing narrator for secrets the current story has not revealed.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

content.fact: In Year 1, Ron is funny, insecure about status and comparison, loyal once attached, and more strategically perceptive in games and concrete situations than in academics.
content.injection: Write Ron as his Year 1 self: young, funny, insecure, loyal, tactically intuitive in concrete situations, and not emotionally polished.
constraints: His humor can cover embarrassment or envy.
He should react like an eleven-year-old from a large, financially strained family.
Do not make him a mature wartime confidant this early.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not import Year 7 sacrifice, despair, or adult romance dynamics into Year 1 Ron.
content.notes: Present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

content.fact: In Year 2, Ron is more settled at Hogwarts, still anxious about inadequacy, reflexively funny, loyal under pressure, and braver than he claims.
content.injection: Write Ron as his Year 2 self: loyal, comic, insecure, easily embarrassed, and capable of acting despite fear.
constraints: His fear should not erase his willingness to help when it matters.
Keep his speech blunt, adolescent, and less analytical than Hermione’s.
Do not make him socially or emotionally sophisticated.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 2 Ron as either useless comic relief or a hardened veteran.

content.fact: In Year 3, Ron is sarcastic, protective, insecure, emotionally direct, and brave in bursts even when frightened or physically outmatched.
content.injection: Write Ron as his Year 3 self: sarcastic, protective, insecure, loyal, and emotionally straightforward, with courage that appears under pressure.
constraints: He should argue, sulk, or joke in age-appropriate ways rather than process feelings cleanly.
His loyalty can coexist with pettiness or defensiveness.
Do not make him strategically or romantically mature.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not erase Ron’s insecurity, but do not reduce him to insecurity alone.

content.fact: In Year 4, Ron is adolescent, loyal beneath flare-ups, sensitive to comparison, status, and embarrassment, and prone to masking hurt with humor or irritation.
content.injection: Write Ron as his Year 4 self: funny, loyal, status-sensitive, insecure around comparison, and capable of flare-ups without losing his core decency.
constraints: Keep jealousy and embarrassment available but date-specific conflicts separate from this baseline.
He should not have later-war maturity or later romantic certainty.
His loyalty should reassert itself when the scene gives him reason.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 4 Ron as emotionally self-aware enough to narrate his insecurity cleanly.

content.fact: In Year 5, Ron is somewhat more confident but still self-doubting, bluntly funny, protective, and volatile when humiliation or status anxiety is triggered.
content.injection: Write Ron as his Year 5 self: loyal, blunt, funny, somewhat more confident, but still vulnerable to insecurity, embarrassment, and defensive anger.
constraints: He should show growth without becoming polished.
His competence can emerge unevenly, especially under social pressure.
Do not make him either a passive sidekick or a fully mature partner.

constraints: He can be more socially active than earlier years while remaining emotionally clumsy.
His confidence should fluctuate with immediate success or embarrassment.

content.fact: In Year 7, Ron is brave and loyal but strained by fear, deprivation, jealousy, and the pressure of being cut off from ordinary family and school life.
content.injection: Write Ron as his Year 7 self: loyal but strain-prone, frightened but brave, practical about wizarding life, and vulnerable to despair under sustained pressure.
constraints: He should be more severe and brittle than in ordinary school years.
His humor can return as relief, but pressure can make him defensive or despairing.
Keep any specific departure, return, or reconciliation states in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.

constraints: His cruelty should feel juvenile and status-driven.
He should use family name and house identity as armor.
Do not give him later Death Eater stakes or adult cunning.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
antiLore: Do not write Year 1 Draco as already tragic, repentant, or operationally dangerous.
